rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference visible ports.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts, avoiding package-qualified names or undefined references.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type annexes for component-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are declared or visible in the current AADL scope to avoid resolution errors.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents or ports in component type contracts; use only visible features.
Do not introduce new data types, packages, or property references that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for AGREE blocks, and ensure all declarations use valid AADL scalar types (int, real, bool).
Use `const`, `eq`, `ass`, and `gua` for constants, equations, assumptions, and guarantees respectively.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
Do not reference event ports or AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables without explicit model support.
